name: Sauteed Corn & Peppers
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse and dry the poblano chile and red bell pepper.
2. Cut a slit near the stem of each pepper.
3. Grill or broil the peppers on a skewer, turning frequently, until the skin begins to blacken, about 8 minutes.
4. Place the peppers in a paper bag to sweat for at least 15 minutes.
5. When cool enough to handle, remove from the bag and discard the skin and seeds.
6. Dice the chili and pepper; set aside.
7. In a large skillet, saute the onion, salt, black pepper, and cumin until the onion is golden, about 5 minutes.
8. Add the corn and saute for 2 minutes.
9. Add the reserved diced chili and pepper and saute for another 3 minutes.
10. Reduce heat and stir in the tomato and lime juice.
11. Serve immediately.
